name: Cheese Filled Babka
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, cream butter with sugar until light and fluffy.
2. In a separate bowl (with mixer at medium speed), beat salt with eggs until they are very thick and pale in color. Stir in the blended butter and sugar mixture.
3. Sprinkle yeast in very warm water (110°F) and stir until dissolved. Beat into the sugar mixture along with lemon rind and cinnamon using a spoon.
4. Add flour, alternately with milk, beating with a spoon after each addition until smooth. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F) until double, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Stir down and let rise again until double, about 45 minutes.
6. Butter a 3-quart fluted ring mold or bundt pan thoroughly, dust with breadcrumbs and set aside.
7. With mixer on high speed, beat together cheese, egg yolks, sugar, and salt until light. Refrigerate.
8. To the Babka dough (second rise):
- Stir dough down and spoon half of it into the prepared mold or bundt pan. Cover with cheese filling, pressing it down into the dough.
- Spoon rest of dough over the filling and let it rise in a warm place until double, about 30 minutes.
9. Heat oven to 350°F. When Babka has doubled, bake for 45-60 minutes or until done. Remove from oven and let stand in pan for 15 minutes. Remove to wire rack and cool completely.
